title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes:
I don't have the steam version. But I can share some tweaks that CAN apply to Proton
Performance is horrendous, the game by nature is very cpu intensive and runs poorly and have stuttering even on Windows
>DIsabling the Vsync and locking the FPS to 60FPS helps a lot to reduce stuttering 
>The OSD from Uplay can cause the game crash so disable it 
>Tweaks that improve the performance a little bit (probably not possible to add in steamplay)
DXVK_LOG_LEVEL none
DXVK_STATE_CACHE 1
DXVK_STATE_CACHE_PATH game directory
PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC 50
WINEDEBUG -all,fixme-all
__GL_NextGenCompiler 1
__GL_SHADER_DISK_CACHE 1
__GL_SHADER_DISK_CACHE_PATH game directory
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Still need to disable the uplay overlay to avoid black screen (why is uplay still around) but the game runs perfectly well. Small aesthetic issue in that resolution detection isn't perfect and all resolutions result in black bars on top and bottom of the screen but functionality is not affected. Played well over ten hours.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: I have the Uplay version of the game, but it runs great. Disable the uplay overlay, and to get rid of the black bars at the top and bottom of the screen, run the game in a wine virtual desktop and the game set to borderless.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes:
I get the Steam version of this game. After download it installs the Uplay and then launch the game, but it's necessary to disable the Uplay overlay in the Uplay settings.
Close the uplay client.
Then launch the game again (inside Steam) now the game will open.
Inside the game go to options and set to borderless, adjust to your screen resolution    and if your hardwares does, set the frame rate to 60 fps.
And it's done, enjoy!

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes:
Has the black bars top and bottom, as mentioned by others. Those do not disappear for me when disabling Uplay overlay. With the overlay enabled, the game will not even start, stuck in a black screen when loading. The only way for me to get rid of the black bars is enabling a desktop environment with a fixed resolution using protontricks. Unfortunately, in that mode my 360 controller is not recognised anymore.

In the end I play with controller and black bars. Occasional crashes are due to my 2 GB of VRAM, which is the official minimum requirement for the game. Would probably happen in Windows as well, but haven't tested it. Apart from this compromise it seems to be fully playable. But a compromise means Silver in my book.

title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes:
To remove the black bars you must enable the option "Emulate the virtual Desktop" in the pfx of the installation. If you have Wine installed, just use this to enable and adjust the screen resolution:


$GAME_ID="812140" WINEPREFIX="$HOME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/${GAME_ID}/pfx/" winecfg

notes: The game runs fine after I disabled the Ubisoft overlay, selected windowed mode (as opposed to fullscreen), and fixed the resolution. notes: Runs really well.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes:
The game starts fullscreen at a very low and weird resolution. I fixed that by going to the settings and making it windowed - only then it recognizes my monitor's correct resolution.

I also disabled the Uplay overlay because I saw it written in other reports but I don't remember if I noticed any changes because of that.

Regarding performance, the game runs pretty much as expected. The FPS seem to be good, between 30 and 55 with graphics set to High.

One of the problems I noticed is that dialogue is bugged. Sometimes the character seems to automatically skip phrases (which hurts the story as I'm trying to pay attention to all the details) or while it's mouthing the selected option, it actually plays the audio for another one.

Also, the game has crashed three times for me already. I remember that one time was when I was drawing the bow - I can't really say if it's connected to that...
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: After UPlay login, the game shows a black screen.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes:
The game runs fine after disabling the Ubisoft overlay.
Performance seems to be stable on "Very High".
I noticed that occasionally the dialog is bugged.
Using virtual desktop to fix black borders breaks controller integration(xbox one). Maxing out the "Screen Display Area"  makes them unobtrusive  -> only some small borders(up,down) remain.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: You must set up a virtual desktop with winecfg with your screen resolution in order to get the display aspect ratio correct. After this it runs mostly perfect, but is missing dialogue in random cutscenes... unfortunately it seems to primarily happen during very important cutscenes making it difficult to recommend unless you don't care at all about the story. Subtitles are also missing during these scenes, not just the audio.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: For anyone struggling with Black screen after game launch, just disable uplay overlay, and for those having trouble with resolution edit "$HOME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/812140/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/Assassin's Creed Odyssey/ACOdyssey.ini" FullscreenWidth/Height and WindowedWidth/Height to the resolution you are aiming for and you should be good to go (Also you might want to set WindowMode to 0 if you can't reach the ingame menu options). Game works flawlessly on Ultra High settings.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

Install winetrick

GAME_ID="812140" WINEPREFIX="$HOME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/${GAME_ID}/pfx/" winecfg

Graphics->Emulate the virtual Desktop: configure with full screen.

title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: The game generally is playable, with some minor disadvantages already mentioned in other comments. Such as missing sounds in cinematics, unable to change graphics settings, minor stutter sometimes. I can change display settings such as resolution or VSync, but I can't change most of Graphics settings (the ones that require restart). By default all graphics settings are set to High, but if I change to Very High or Ultra High then game asks to restart, and after starting a restart dies and no longer launches. After this I need to delete config from prefix... I would consider bronze, but I am amazed by Photon reaching this level in such a short amount of time, it deserves Silver!
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Since Proton 4.11-3, the game doesn't launch anymore. I tried to reinstall all (Steam + ACOdyssey + Proton) but nothing more. I also tried with protontricks 812140 uplay but it doesn't work either.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes:
I was able to get the voices and subtitles working (had some saves at cinematic moments) by disabling Esync. After this game seems solid. very playable.

As previous comments have said, 
* Don't run the game with Uplay overlay
* Use winecfg to emulate the virtual desktop for full screen
* Disable Esync (PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%)
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: The game starts and is fully playable with kernel option "clearcpuid=514" for Ryzen 3xxx CPU hardware.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Firstly, the performance of the game on my hardware is good and about what I observed on Windows. Although I will likely post an update once ACO compiler merges into the stable Mesa release.  I had to use Winetricks/protontricks to enable virtual desktop to fix the incorrect resolution reading (this step isnt necessary, but otherwise the resolution gets set to its absolute lowest by default). Also, if Uplay overlay is not disabled then it can cause the game to hang on a black screen. I have also observed that issue on Windows. Occasionally during Cutscenes the audio stops working. This is annoying, but can be avoided by saving regularly or before talking to people. In happened only once in about eight hour of gameplay. I want to give it a gold rating, but the issues are not fixed as of yet.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Disabled UPlay overlay, works beautifully, minor texture and speech glitching every once in a while but the game plays excellently.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Game work mostly fine when disabling UPlay overlay and after using winecfg to fix the fullscreen issue. I rate as Silver because there is a glitch with the sound during cinematic. It does not happen very often and could be considered as minor but in my opinion it is enough to break the immersion in the game.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey
